hero_subtitle: SFTY connects MAS-specific KYT monitoring with provider orchestration to streamline compliance workflows, preserve audit evidence, and support regulated VASPs across jurisdictions.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling

problem_title: KYT monitoring remains disconnected from MAS workflows
problem_description: Compliance teams struggle to integrate jurisdiction-specific MAS KYT requirements into existing digital asset monitoring processes. This separation slows risk detection, weakens audit trails, and complicates defensible compliance under MAS regulatory expectations.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY unifies wallet risk signals, transaction alerts, and jurisdictional MAS requirements into one compliance workflow that supports case management, evidence preservation, rule updates, and audit-ready reporting for VASPs.
workflow_title: From wallet risk to MAS compliance review
workflow_description: SFTY aggregates KYT wallet risk alerts, routes them through jurisdiction-specific reviews, opens cases, records decisions and preserves evidence for MAS compliance workflows.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Beyond fragmented checks. One jurisdictional layer
why_sfty_description: SFTY connects MAS-specific KYT outputs into structured workflows, cases, decisions and evidence to unify compliance across jurisdictional rules and operational requirements.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY supports VASPs managing MAS-specific KYT workflows with structured provider orchestration, audit evidence preservation and enterprise-ready deployment controls.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance
ask_sfty_placeholder: How can SFTY support MAS KYT compliance workflows
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how MAS KYT alerts integrate into case workflows
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how audit evidence supports MAS regulatory reviews
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Compare provider orchestration for MAS-specific KYT monitoring
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for MAS compliance teams?
faq_1_answer: SFTY provides MAS compliance teams a connected platform to connect KYT data, regulatory rules, cases and reports into structured workflows tailored for Singapore's jurisdiction-specific requirements.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY support MAS KYT workflows?
faq_2_answer: SFTY structures MAS KYT workflows by aggregating wallet and transaction risks, routing alerts, applying MAS rules, creating cases and linking them to regulatory obligations in one operational layer.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve MAS audit evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves audit evidence by maintaining decision logs, case histories and provider outputs for MAS regulatory reviews, supporting traceability and defensible documentation across compliance operations.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate existing MAS KYT providers?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY connects and orchestrates existing MAS-approved KYT and screening providers’ outputs into a single compliance workflow without replacing or duplicating underlying provider functions.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace MAS KYT or Travel Rule providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY does not replace MAS KYT or Travel Rule providers but complements them by turning their outputs into structured cases, evidence and reporting workflows workflows.
final_title: Connect MAS KYT monitoring to compliance workflows
final_description: Review how SFTY structures MAS-specific KYT alerts into controlled cases with audit-ready evidence and provider orchestration.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.

footer_legal_notice: This page is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal, regulatory or compliance advice. SFTY deployment depends on jurisdiction, client requirements and applicable laws.
